what_it_does: Node.js CLI wrapper for an MCP server. It verifies that required runtime dependencies are present under node_modules (using a helper from ./install-check.js), runs npm install if anything is missing, checks that dist/mcp-server.js exists, then spawns the actual MCP server with inherited stdio. It forwards SIGTERM/SIGINT/SIGHUP to the child, kills the child when stdin closes (parent-death detection), and propagates the child's exit code or signal.
observations: Uses ESM imports and a shebang for direct execution.
Determines plugin root from CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT or falls back to the parent of the script directory.
Invokes findMissingDeps to probe package manifests, not just the node_modules folder, to catch partial extractions.
Runs npm install --no-audit --no-fund with platform-specific npm/npm.cmd and shell handling.
Redirects npm install stdout/stderr to process.stderr to avoid cluttering MCP logs.
Spawns the server with process.execPath and shell: false.
Forwards SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP to the child process.
Detects parent process death via process.stdin 'end' and exits after killing the child.
Propagates child exit by signal or code.
standouts: Self-healing dependency installation before server start.
Partial-extraction bug fix comment referencing issue #95.
Parent-death detection through stdin close rather than explicit heartbeat.
conventions: Spawn with stdio: 'inherit' for transparent child I/O.
Signal forwarding from parent to child.
concerns: SIGHUP forwarding is not meaningful on Windows and may be ignored or error.
process.stdin 'end' may not fire reliably in all parent/child execution contexts.
npm install has no timeout, so a hung install blocks server startup indefinitely.
runNpmInstall uses shell: true on Windows, which can have security implications if inputs were untrusted, though here they are fixed.
child.kill() without an explicit signal defaults to SIGTERM, which may not terminate a stuck server.
notes: A cross-platform, self-healing bootstrap that makes sure dependencies and the compiled server exist before proxying control and lifecycle signals to the real MCP server.
questions: What exact dependency list does findMissingDeps check?
How is dist/mcp-server.js produced and what build step is expected?
Is CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT documented or set by the host environment?
Does stdin-end detection work reliably when the host closes the connection on Windows?
What happens if npm install partially succeeds but still leaves dependencies missing?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a cross-platform wrapper that ensures dependencies are installed and then starts the MCP server.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the MCP server runnable with zero manual setup by auto-repairing missing dependencies and acting as a transparent lifecycle proxy between the host and the server process.
novelty: The dependency check probes individual package manifests rather than just node_modules existence, explicitly to catch partial extractions, and it couples that with parent-death detection via stdin closure.
contribution: A bootstrap layer that can detect and repair missing dependencies, validate the server artifact, spawn the server transparently, and forward termination signals and parent-death events.
summary_for_embedding: A Node.js CLI wrapper that checks for missing runtime dependencies, runs npm install if needed, then spawns the compiled MCP server and forwards signals and parent-death events to it.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a cross-platform, self-healing entry point that ensures the MCP server starts with all dependencies present and responds correctly to process lifecycle events.

symbols.does: Spawns npm install in the plugin root and resolves when it succeeds or rejects on failure.
symbols.description: runNpmInstall is a helper that runs npm install --no-audit --no-fund inside PLUGIN_ROOT. It chooses npm or npm.cmd based on the platform, streams npm output to stderr, and returns a promise that resolves on exit code 0 or rejects with an error otherwise.

symbols.does: Checks dependencies, installs missing ones, validates the server binary, spawns it, and manages child lifecycle.
symbols.description: main is the async entry point. It calls findMissingDeps to detect missing runtime dependencies and runs npm install if any are found. It then verifies dist/mcp-server.js exists, spawns it with process.execPath, forwards SIGTERM/SIGINT/SIGHUP, kills the child when stdin ends, and propagates the child's exit status or signal.
